reasoning: In the May 2025 FIDE rankings, Polina Shuvalova is ranked exactly 11th with a rating of 2494. However, she is in a vulnerable 3-way tie for the 11th–13th spots with Bibisara Assaubayeva and Mariya Muzychuk. Furthermore, the gap to overtake the players above her (Nana Dzagnidze at 2509, Kateryna Lagno at 2516) is significant (15+ points), while the gap to the players below her is very small (Harika Dronavalli at 2483). Because FIDE rankings update monthly and are subject to tie-break rules and minor rating fluctuations, any perturbation (such as one of the tied players gaining even a single point, or Harika having a good tournament) will likely push Shuvalova out of the top 11. The asymmetry of the rating gaps means downward movement in rank is much more probable in the short term than upward movement. Thus, the probability she maintains a rank of 11 or higher is below 50%. Over the longer horizons (August and November), the natural variance increases and Shuvalova, who is young and has recently been on an upward trajectory, has a better chance of pushing past the 2500 barrier into the top 10. As per instructions, estimates reflect uncertainty regressing toward 0.5 over time.
